rulings.Case Title: PRABHABEN PATEL VS. CARL F. WILLGEROTH et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Compel Further Responses To Req For Admissions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, July 31, 2012, Line 1, PLAINTIFF PRABHABEN PATEL's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Admissions. Pro Tem Judge Trent Thornley, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ANNA FLIDER VS. JOSEPH DUGAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn For Relief Fron Judgmnt
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, July 31, 2012, Line 2, DEFENDANT NOE STREET PROPERTY, L.P.'S MOTION For Relief Fron JudgmENT. The court adopts the recommendation of Pro Tem Thornley as follows: DENIED. Moving party seeks relief under the mandatory provisions of CCP § 473 from an order granting summary judgment against it. Moving party argues the summary judgment effectively constitutes a "default judgment" because the court denied its application for joinder with another defendant's opposition, thereby leaving moving party without any opposition. In so doing, the court relied upon previous orders imposing and sustaining evidentiary sanctions against moving party. Moving party relies on Avila v. Chua (1997) 57 Cal.App.4th 860, in which the lower court struck a late-filed opposition, granted summary judgment, and denied opposing party's application for relief under §473. The appellate court reversed, holding that the summary judgment in those circumstances effectively constituted a default judgment against opposing party. A party should not lose its day in court solely due to counsel's admitted error in failing to timely file opposition papers. However, Avila does not apply for at least two reasons. First, Avila itself noted that the "mandatory provisions [of §473] do not apply where there has been a hearing in which the court has evaluated the excuses for delay." Id. at 868. Here, moving party's alleged attorney error and excuses for delay in responding to discovery have been litigated fully. In addition to the hearing on the sanctions, the court also heard and denied moving party's motion for reconsideration of the sanctions. Second, in Avila, §473 was applied to an attorney mistake of tardy court filing, rending the circumstances in Avila more closely akin to a default judgment resulting from attorney error. Here, moving party essentially contests the court's reliance on its prior orders in denying its application for joinder. Moving party cites Le Francois v. Goel (2005) 35 Cal.4th 1094, 1107, arguing the court may reconsider its prior rulings sua sponte. The court declines to do so. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/CK)

rulings.Case Title: MATEEL ENVIRONMENTAL JUSTICE FOUNDATION VS. ANTHROPOLGIE INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Oiton To Approve Prop 65 Settlement And For Entry Of Consent Judgment As To Anthropologie, Inc.
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, July 31, 2012, Line 8, PLAINTIFF MATEEL ENVIRONMENTAL JUSTICE FOUNDATION'S MOTion To Approve PropOSITION 65 Settlement And For Entry Of Consent Judgment As To Anthropologie, Inc.. Denied without prejudice. (1) The materials supplied are insufficient to approve the reasonableness of the attorneys fees. Specifically, while there is a basis for the hourly rate, there is nothing to suggest that the hours spent on e.g. 'litigation' (or any other category) were reasonable. Simply breaking down hours into large categories such as 'litigation,' 'investigation' and 'post settlement' does not permit any judgment of reasonableness. (2) It appears that the total fines are $18,000. It does not appear that the agreement provides the correct percentage of that sum (75%) to the state. (3) It does not appear that releasing language in the settlement agreement states that no claims arising after the date of the judgment are released. =(302/CK)

rulings.Case Title: VANDANA UPADHYAY VS. FARISSE LAW FIRM et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, July 31, 2012, Line 13, DEFENDANTS FARISSE LAW FIRM, SIMONA FARISSE, CARLA MINNARD'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Sustained without leave to amend. The claim is time-barred. The allegations in the First Amended Complaint demonstrate Plaintiff knew, on May 3, 2010, she released all her rights to any employee benefit plans pursuant to the settlement agreement. The statute of limitations period was, therefore, triggered on May 3, 2010 and expired on May 3, 2011. The actual injury suffered by Plaintiff was the loss of benefits which occurred upon execution of the settlement agreement on May 3, 2010 thus the statute of limitations time was not tolled. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/CK)

rulings.Case Title: CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO VS. ANSHEN + ALLEN, ARCHITECTS, INC. et al*****TRANSFERRED TO ALAMEDA COUNTY*****
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings As To Stantec'S X-Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, July 31, 2012, Line 14, CROSS DEFENDANT TURNER CONSTRUCTION COMPANY, INC.'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings As To Stantec'S CROSS-Complaint. Sustained with ten days leave to amend. Cross-Complainant Stantec is granted leave to amend, if it can do so in good faith, to allege facts which show Cross-Defendant Turner owes Cross-Complainant a duty. Without contractual privity, it appears that a special relationship between Turner and Stantec must be pleaded. J'Aire Corp. v. Gregory, 24 Cal.3d 799 (1979). Generally that special relationship is not found as between construction managers and architects. Ratcliff Architects v. Vanir Contruction Mangement, Inc., 88 Cal.App.4th 595 (2001). The present complaint alleges that Turner owned a duty to Stantec but does not plead facts to establish that duty, such as the Biakanja factors discussed in J'Aire. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/CK)

The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: CATALINO CARINO et al VS. ALIREZA KATOUZIAN MD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Transfer Venue [Ccp 397]; Memo P/A; Declarations
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, July 31, 2012, Line 15, DEFENDANT DIGNITY HEALTH DBA ST. JOHN'S REGIONAL MEDICAL, DIGNITY HEALTH'S Motion To Transfer Venue [Ccp 397]. Denied without prejudice. It does not appear that the fees required by CCP § 399 have been posted; these are required because the motion is made under § 397 (c). If such fees have been posted counsel should appear at the hearing with a declaration under penalty of perjury to that effect. See generally W. Rylaarsdam, et al., CALIFORNIA PRACTICE GUIDE: CIVIL PROCEDURE BEFORE TRIAL § 3:578 (Rutter Group 2011). [On the merits, it appears that transfer could be ordered, because there are what appear to be at least 6 non party witnesses for whom venue in Ventura would be far more convenient than San Francisco, and there is no showing that for other non-party witnesses venue is more convenient in San Francisco. The Court notes the Opposition's suggestion that the identified witnesses are in fact party witnesses, and this issue must be clarified if any further motion to transfer venue were to be brought. There is no showing that trial would be delayed if venue were to be transferred.] Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling (not that within brackets) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/CK)

rulings.Case Title: SERIES GROUP LLC A LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY VS. UNIQUE MANAGEMENT ASSOCIATES, INC. A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion To Compel
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, July 31, 2012, Line 7, PLAINTIFF SERIES GROUP LLC A LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY's Motion To Compel. Pro Tem Judge Trent Thornley, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: NATALLIA NESTSIAROVICH VS. UNITED PARCEL SVC, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notcie Of Motion To Transfer Venue
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, July 31, 2012, Line 19, DEFENDANTS UNITED PARCEL SERVICE, INC., LISA GONZALES' NotICe Of Motion To Transfer Venue. Denied. Defendant United Parcel Service failed to show San Francisco County is an improper venue. Furthermore, United Parcel Service has not identified the location or existence of its principal place of business in California. Thus, venue is proper in any county in California. Easton v. Superior Court (1970) 12 Cal.App.3d 243, 246-247. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/CK)
